findings.title: Known vulnerable dependency versions in the locked tree
findings.root_cause: Framework, networking, image, WebSocket, and build-tool versions had accumulated vulnerable transitive releases and CI did not enforce an audit.
findings.impact: Known dependency flaws could weaken reachable request, parsing, or tooling paths; no active exploitation or credential exposure was found.
findings.remediation: Upgrade direct dependencies, override only affected transitive versions, refresh the lockfile, and enforce a production audit in CI.
findings.validation_test: npm audit --audit-level=low and npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high both report zero vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Public RPC route allowed unrestricted upstream method relay
findings.root_cause: The relay treated arbitrary JSON as trusted RPC input and delegated policy enforcement to the upstream.
findings.impact: The public route could be abused as a proxy, consume quota and worker resources, or expose unintended RPC methods.
findings.remediation: Allowlist read/simulation methods and enforce origin, schema, body, response, timeout, rate, and upstream URL controls.
findings.validation_test: npm test verifies unsafe methods, batches, and cross-origin requests are rejected.

findings.title: Portfolio aggregation lacked resource and provider-target controls
findings.root_cause: External indexers and administrator-supplied provider configuration were assumed to be well behaved.
findings.impact: Repeated calls or stalled and oversized responses could consume worker capacity and provider quota; unsafe configuration could target an unintended host.
findings.remediation: Add trusted-edge throttling, timeouts, size caps, strict JSON parsing, HTTPS/private-host validation, bounded normalization, and a global Vercel WAF rule.
findings.validation_test: npm test covers invalid requests and Vercel edge identity; the published firewall rule covers both public API routes.

findings.title: Browser responses lacked a global security-header policy
findings.root_cause: Security headers were not centralized at the final Worker response boundary.
findings.impact: Browser defense in depth against clickjacking, injection, MIME confusion, referrer leakage, and device access was weaker than necessary.
findings.remediation: Attach a shared CSP, anti-framing, nosniff, permissions, referrer, cross-origin, and HSTS policy to Cloudflare and native Next.js responses.
findings.validation_test: Rendered integration tests assert the headers; both npm run build and npm run build:vercel pass.

findings.title: Ritual LLM accepted mutable model selection and untrusted portfolio text without explicit prompt boundaries
findings.root_cause: Executor configuration and fetched content crossed the AI trust boundary without an allowlisted model or defensive system instruction.
findings.impact: Malicious asset metadata could influence decision-support text and arbitrary models could make behavior and cost less predictable.
findings.remediation: Pin the registered model, mark portfolio content as untrusted data, ignore embedded commands, and JSON-escape control characters.
findings.validation_test: The Hardhat test rejects a non-allowlisted model and npm run ritual:check confirms registration.

findings.title: Ritual storage and scheduling inputs were insufficiently bounded
findings.root_cause: The contract relied on downstream protocol behavior and transaction gas instead of application-level size and schedule invariants.
findings.impact: Oversized executor output or excessive schedules could cause avoidable reverts, cost, storage growth, or degraded availability.
findings.remediation: Bound portfolio, analysis, error, URL, and schedule values and validate wallet/executor addresses.
findings.validation_test: Hardhat compilation and contract tests pass and the secured artifact is deployed on Ritual testnet.

findings.title: One-step ownership transfer could irreversibly misdirect administration
findings.root_cause: Administrative transfer did not verify that the destination address was correct and controllable.
findings.impact: A typo or incompatible destination could permanently lose access to endpoint, scheduler, and fee management.
findings.remediation: Use nomination plus acceptance by the pending owner.
findings.validation_test: The Hardhat test confirms nomination and acceptance semantics.

findings.title: On-chain LLM output was trusted through a TypeScript assertion
findings.owasp_category: A08:2021 — Software and Data Integrity Failures
findings.root_cause: A compile-time TypeScript assertion was used at an untrusted runtime boundary.
findings.impact: Malformed model output could break rendering or display structurally misleading content; no script-injection sink was confirmed.
findings.remediation: Validate exact keys, ranges, enums, lengths, and array counts before rendering.
findings.validation_test: tests/security.test.ts accepts a valid result and rejects malformed and oversized results.

findings.title: Request-derived host data could influence canonical metadata
findings.root_cause: Request routing metadata and canonical public-site identity were conflated.
findings.impact: A misconfigured proxy could generate social or canonical URLs for an attacker-selected host.
findings.remediation: Use a validated HTTPS site URL with a fixed trusted fallback.
findings.validation_test: npm run build generates the application successfully with the bounded metadata configuration.
